title: entada gigas subsp. rectocarpa
intro: Entada gigas subsp. rectocarpa, also known as the "devil's horse", is a plant species that belongs to the legume family and is native to certain regions in Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antiseptic effects. The plant has been associated with several health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues. In herbal preparations, Entada gigas subsp. rectocarpa is often used in decoctions, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although its use should be cautious and under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to potential interactions and side effects.
